output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰӨаҰҫаҰ№аҰІа§Ү аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰҮ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°а§ҮаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰҫаҰӘаҰҹа§Ү аҰ•а§ӢаҰЎ аҰёаҰӮаҰ¶а§ӢаҰ§аҰЁ аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰӘаҰ°а§ҖаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҜаҰја§ӢаҰңаҰЁа§ҖаҰҜаҰј аҰ®а§ӢаҰҹ CPU аҰҳаҰЈа§ҚаҰҹаҰҫ аҰ–а§ҒаҰҒаҰңа§Ү аҰ¬а§ҮаҰ° аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§ҮаҘӨ аҰҰаҰҫаҰҒаҰЎаҰјаҰҫаҰЁ, аҰ•аҰҝаҰЁа§ҚаҰӨа§Ғ аҰ…аҰӘа§ҮаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°а§ҒаҰЁ, аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶а§ҚаҰЁаҰҹаҰҝ аҰ•аҰҝ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•а§ҢаҰ¶аҰІа§Җ аҰёаҰ®аҰёа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫ? аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•а§ҢаҰ¶аҰІа§Җ аҰёаҰ®аҰёа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰҜаҰј, аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҜаҰја§ӢаҰңаҰЁа§ҖаҰҜаҰј аҰ®а§ӢаҰҹ аҰ•аҰҫаҰңа§ҮаҰ° аҰёаҰ®аҰҜаҰј аҰёаҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰ°аҰЈаҰӨ аҰ•аҰҫаҰңа§ҮаҰ° аҰ§аҰҫаҰӘаҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§ӢаҰ° аҰүаҰӘаҰ° аҰӯаҰҝаҰӨа§ҚаҰӨаҰҝ аҰ•аҰ°а§Ү аҰ—аҰЈаҰЁаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰјаҘӨ аҰёа§ҒаҰӨаҰ°аҰҫаҰӮ, аҰҜаҰҰаҰҝ аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ• аҰ•а§ӢаҰЎ аҰёаҰӮаҰ¶а§ӢаҰ§аҰЁ аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰӘаҰ°а§ҖаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҜаҰја§ӢаҰңаҰЁа§ҖаҰҜаҰј аҰ®а§ӢаҰҹ аҰёаҰ®аҰҜаҰј аҰңаҰҫаҰЁаҰӨа§Ү аҰҡаҰҫаҰҜаҰј, аҰӨаҰҫаҰ№аҰІа§Ү аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰёа§ҮаҰҮ аҰ•аҰҫаҰңа§ҮаҰ° аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӨаҰҝаҰҹаҰҝ аҰ§аҰҫаҰӘа§ҮаҰ° аҰёаҰ®аҰҜаҰј аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¬аҰ°а§ҚаҰӨаҰЁ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§Ү аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰёа§ҮаҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§ӢаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰ•аҰёаҰҫаҰҘа§Ү аҰҜа§ӢаҰ— аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§ҮаҘӨ аҰ•аҰҝаҰЁа§ҚаҰӨпҝҪ

title: Resolving Indirect Referring Expressions for Entity Selection
abstract: Recent advances in language modeling have enabled new conversational systems. In particular, it is often desirable for people to make choices among specified options when using such systems. We address the problem of reference resolution, when people use natural expressions to choose between real world entities. For example, given the choice вҖҳShould we make a Simnel cake or a Pandan cakeВҝ a natural response from a non-expert may be indirect: вҖҳletвҖҷs make the green oneвҖҳ. Reference resolution has been little studied with natural expressions, thus robustly understanding such language has large potential for improving naturalness in dialog, recommendation, and search systems. We create AltEntities (Alternative Entities), a new public dataset of entity pairs and utterances, and develop models for the disambiguation problem. Consisting of 42K indirect referring expressions across three domains, it enables for the first time the study of how large language models can be adapted to this task. We find they achieve 82%-87% accuracy in realistic settings, which while reasonable also invites further advances.
question: AltEntities dataset-аҰҸ аҰ•аҰӨаҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§Ӣ alternative questions аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰ•аҰӨаҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§Ӣ referring expressions (REs) аҰ°а§ҹа§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?

output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰӨаҰҫаҰ№аҰІа§Ү аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰёа§ҮаҰҹаҰҝ аҰ–а§ҒаҰҒаҰңа§Ү аҰ¬а§ҮаҰ° аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§Ү: Hosseini, Mohammad Javad аҰҸаҰ° аҰӘаҰҫаҰ а§ҚаҰҜаҰӘа§ҒаҰёа§ҚаҰӨаҰ•а§ҮаҰ° "Resolving Indirect Referring Expressions for Entity Selection" аҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰ…аҰӮаҰ¶а§Ү, аҰ…аҰІ-аҰҸаҰЁа§ҚаҰҹаҰҫаҰ°аҰҝаҰёа§ҚаҰҹ аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҮаҰҹа§Ү аҰ•аҰӨаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝ аҰ…а§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰІа§ҚаҰҹаҰҫаҰ°а§ҚаҰЁа§ҮаҰҹаҰҝаҰӯ аҰңаҰҝаҰӯ аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰ•аҰӨаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӨаҰҝаҰ¶аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҰ аҰүаҰІа§ҚаҰІа§ҮаҰ– аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§ҮаҘӨ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰёа§ҚаҰ®аҰ°аҰЈ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁ аҰҜа§Ү аҰ…аҰІ-аҰҸаҰЁа§ҚаҰҹаҰҫаҰ°аҰҝаҰёа§ҚаҰҹ аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҮаҰҹ аҰ•а§ҖаҘӨ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ° аҰҜаҰӨаҰҰа§ӮаҰ° аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰӘаҰЎаҰја§Ү, аҰҸаҰҹаҰҝ аҰ№аҰІа§Ӣ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰӨаҰҘа§ҚаҰҜ аҰ—а§ҚаҰ°аҰ№аҰЈ аҰ•а§ҢаҰ¶аҰІ аҰӘаҰ°аҰҝаҰ®аҰҫаҰӘа§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰӘаҰ°аҰҝаҰҡаҰҝаҰӨ аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫ аҰёа§ҮаҰҹаҘӨ аҰҸаҰҹаҰҝ аҰёаҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰ°аҰЈаҰӨ аҰ…а§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰІа§ҚаҰҹаҰҫаҰ°а§ҚаҰЁа§ҮаҰҹаҰҝаҰӯ аҰңаҰҝаҰӯ (аҰ…а§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰІа§ҚаҰҹаҰҫаҰ°а§ҚаҰЁа§ҮаҰҹаҰҝаҰӯ аҰңаҰҝаҰӯ аҰ…а§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰЁа§ҚаҰҹаҰҝаҰё) аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӨаҰҝаҰ¶аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҰ аҰүаҰІа§ҚаҰІа§ҮаҰ– аҰ•аҰ°а§ҮаҘӨ аҰёа§ҒаҰӨаҰ°аҰҫаҰӮ, аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӯаҰ¬аҰӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӨаҰҝаҰҹаҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶а§ҚаҰЁа§Ү аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӨаҰҝаҰ¶аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҰ аҰ°аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү аҰҜаҰҫ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰёа§ҚаҰ¬аҰӨаҰЁа§ҚаҰӨа§ҚаҰ° аҰңаҰҝаҰӯа§ҮаҰ° аҰёаҰҫаҰҘа§Ү аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӘаҰ°

title: WikiBio: a Semantic Resource for the Intersectional Analysis of Biographical Events
abstract: Biographical event detection is a relevant task that allows for the exploration and comparison of the ways in which peopleвҖҷs lives are told and represented. This may support several real-life applications in digital humanities and in works aimed at exploring bias about minoritized groups. Despite that, there are no corpora and models specifically designed for this task. In this paper we fill this gap by presenting a new corpus annotated for biographical event detection. The corpus, which includes 20 Wikipedia biographies, was aligned with 5 existing corpora in order to train a model for the biographical event detection task. The model was able to detect all mentions of the target-entity in a biography with an F-score of 0.808 and the entity-related events with an F-score of 0.859. Finally, the model was used for performing an analysis of biases about women and non-Western people in Wikipedia biographies.
question: WikiBio аҰ•аҰ°а§ҚаҰӘаҰҫаҰёа§Ү аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰңаҰҫаҰӨаҰҝаҰ—аҰӨ аҰӘаҰ°аҰҝаҰҡаҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ…аҰЁа§ҚаҰӨаҰ°а§ҚаҰӯа§ҒаҰ•а§ҚаҰӨ аҰҶаҰӣа§ҮаҰЁ?

title: Hybrid Transducer and Attention based Encoder-Decoder Modeling for Speech-to-Text Tasks
abstract: Transducer and Attention based Encoder-Decoder (AED) are two widely used frameworks for speech-to-text tasks. They are designed for different purposes and each has its own benefits and drawbacks for speech-to-text tasks. In order to leverage strengths of both modeling methods, we propose a solution by combining Transducer and Attention based Encoder-Decoder (TAED) for speech-to-text tasks. The new method leverages AEDвҖҷs strength in non-monotonic sequence to sequence learning while retaining TransducerвҖҷs streaming property. In the proposed framework, Transducer and AED share the same speech encoder. The predictor in Transducer is replaced by the decoder in the AED model, and the outputs of the decoder are conditioned on the speech inputs instead of outputs from an unconditioned language model. The proposed solution ensures that the model is optimized by covering all possible read/write scenarios and creates a matched environment for streaming applications. We evaluate the proposed approach on the MuST-C dataset and the findings demonstrate that TAED performs significantly better than Transducer for offline automatic speech recognition (ASR) and speech-to-text translation (ST) tasks. In the streaming case, TAED outperforms Transducer in the ASR task and one ST direction while comparable results are achieved in another translation direction.
question: аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰҰа§ҒаҰҹаҰҝ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҫ-аҰңа§ӢаҰЎаҰјаҰҫаҰ° аҰүаҰӘаҰ° аҰ•аҰҫаҰң аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№а§ҹа§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?
prompt: "Hybrid Transducer and Attention based Encoder-Decoder Modeling for Speech-to-Text Tasks" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰҜаҰј Tang,Yun  et al. аҰҰа§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ, аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰҰа§ҒаҰҹаҰҝ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҫ-аҰңа§ӢаҰЎаҰјаҰҫаҰ° аҰүаҰӘаҰ° аҰ•аҰҫаҰң аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№а§ҹа§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?
output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰӨаҰҫаҰҮ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰҮ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°а§ҮаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰҫаҰӘаҰҹа§Ү, "Hybrid Transducer and Attention based Encoder-Decoder Modeling for Speech-to-Text Tasks" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰҹаҰҝаҰ° аҰёаҰҫаҰҘа§Ү, аҰҜа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰЁа§Ү аҰ…аҰЁа§ҒаҰ¬аҰҫаҰҰ аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰ…аҰЁа§ҒаҰ¬аҰҫаҰҰ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҫаҰ° аҰңа§ӢаҰЎаҰјаҰҫаҰ° аҰүаҰӘаҰ° аҰ•аҰҫаҰң аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү, аҰӨаҰҫ аҰЁаҰҝаҰ°а§ҚаҰ§аҰҫаҰ°аҰЈ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§ҮаҘӨ аҰ№а§ҒаҰ®аҰ®, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹа§Ғ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ¬аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁаҘӨ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ° аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү аҰҜа§Ү аҰҸаҰҮ аҰ§аҰ°аҰЁа§ҮаҰ° аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°а§ҮаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰҫаҰӘаҰҹа§Ү, аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰёаҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰ°аҰЈаҰӨ аҰҰа§ҒаҰҹаҰҝ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҫ аҰЁаҰҝаҰ°а§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰҡаҰЁ аҰ•аҰ°а§ҮаҰЁ, аҰҜа§ҮаҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§ӢаҰӨа§Ү аҰӨаҰҫаҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰ•а§ҢаҰ¶аҰІ аҰӘаҰ°а§ҖаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰҜа§ҮаҰӨа§Ү аҰӘаҰҫаҰ°а§Ү аҰ…аҰҘаҰ¬аҰҫ аҰҜа§ҮаҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§ӢаҰӨа§Ү аҰӨаҰҫаҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰӘаҰҰа§ҚаҰ§аҰӨаҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӯаҰҫаҰ¬аҰҝаҰӨ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰҜа§ҮаҰӨа§Ү аҰӘаҰҫаҰ°а§ҮаҘӨ аҰҸаҰ–аҰҫаҰЁа§Ү аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰҰа§ҒаҰҹаҰҝ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҫ аҰңа§ӢаҰЎаҰјаҰҫаҰ° аҰ•аҰҘаҰҫ аҰ¬аҰІаҰҫ аҰ№аҰҡа§ҚаҰӣа§Ү? аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҶаҰ¬аҰҫаҰ° аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°а§ҮаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰҫаҰӘаҰҹаҰҹаҰҝ аҰҰа§ҮаҰ–аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁаҘӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҘаҰ®аҰӨ, аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ• аҰ№аҰІа§Ӣ аҰҹаҰҫаҰҷ, аҰҜаҰҫаҰ° аҰӘаҰ°а§Ү аҰ…аҰЁа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ

title: WhatвҖҷs the Meaning of Superhuman Performance in TodayвҖҷs NLU?
abstract: In the last five years, there has been a significant focus in Natural Language Processing (NLP) on developing larger Pretrained Language Models (PLMs) and introducing benchmarks such as SuperGLUE and SQuAD to measure their abilities in language understanding, reasoning, and reading comprehension. These PLMs have achieved impressive results on these benchmarks, even surpassing human performance in some cases. This has led to claims of superhuman capabilities and the provocative idea that certain tasks have been solved. In this position paper, we take a critical look at these claims and ask whether PLMs truly have superhuman abilities and what the current benchmarks are really evaluating. We show that these benchmarks have serious limitations affecting the comparison between humans and PLMs and provide recommendations for fairer and more transparent benchmarks.
question: SuperGLUE-аҰҸаҰ° аҰ•а§ҹаҰҹаҰҝ аҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҚаҰ•а§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰ®аҰҫаҰЁаҰ¬ аҰ•аҰ°а§ҚаҰ®аҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰ®аҰӨаҰҫ аҰёаҰӮаҰ¶а§ҚаҰІаҰҝаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹ аҰӘа§ҮаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ—аҰЈаҰЁаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°а§ҮаҰӣа§ҮаҰЁ ?
prompt: "WhatвҖҷs the Meaning of Superhuman Performance in TodayвҖҷs NLU?" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰҜаҰј Tedeschi,Simone  et al. аҰҰа§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ, superGLUE-аҰҸаҰ° аҰ•а§ҹаҰҹаҰҝ аҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҚаҰ•а§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰ®аҰҫаҰЁаҰ¬ аҰ•аҰ°а§ҚаҰ®аҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰ®аҰӨаҰҫ аҰёаҰӮаҰ¶а§ҚаҰІаҰҝаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹ аҰӘа§ҮаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ—аҰЈаҰЁаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°а§ҮаҰӣа§ҮаҰЁ ?
output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰӨаҰҫаҰ№аҰІа§Ү аҰҸаҰҮ аҰёаҰ®аҰёа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰҹаҰҝ аҰ№аҰІ: аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ•аҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰ•а§ҮаҰЁ superGLUE-аҰҸаҰ° аҰ•аҰҜаҰјаҰҹаҰҝ аҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҚаҰ•а§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰ®аҰҫаҰЁаҰ¬ аҰ•аҰ°а§ҚаҰ®аҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰ®аҰӨаҰҫ аҰ—аҰЈаҰЁаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰӣаҰҝаҰІ аҰӨаҰҫ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰ¬а§ҮаҰ° аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§Ү аҰҜа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰЁа§Ү Tedeschi, Simone аҰҮаҰӨа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰҰаҰҝ аҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰӘа§ҮаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°а§ҮаҘӨ аҰ№а§ҒаҰ®аҰ®, superGLUE-аҰҸаҰ° аҰ•аҰҜаҰјаҰҹаҰҝ аҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҚаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү аҰӨаҰҫ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰёа§ҚаҰ®аҰ°аҰЈ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§Ү аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӯаҰ¬аҰӨ аҰӘа§ҮаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°а§Ү аҰ•аҰҝаҰӣа§Ғ аҰүаҰІа§ҚаҰІа§ҮаҰ– аҰ…аҰҘаҰ¬аҰҫ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӯаҰ¬аҰӨ аҰҸаҰҹаҰҝ аҰЁаҰҝаҰ°а§ҚаҰҰаҰҝаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰҶаҰӣа§ҮаҘӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҘаҰ®аҰӨ, superGLUE аҰ•аҰҝ аҰ•а§Җ? аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ° аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү аҰҜа§Ү superGLUE аҰ№аҰІа§Ӣ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰ®а§ҒаҰ•а§ҚаҰӨ аҰӘаҰ°аҰҝаҰёаҰӮаҰ№аҰҫаҰ°аҰЁа§ҖаҰҜаҰј аҰӘаҰ°а§ҖаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰҫ аҰёаҰҝаҰёа§ҚаҰҹа§ҮаҰ® аҰҜаҰҫ аҰ¬аҰҝаҰӯаҰҝаҰЁа§ҚаҰЁ аҰ—аҰҫаҰЈаҰҝаҰӨаҰҝаҰ• аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ¶а§ҚаҰІа§ҮаҰ·аҰЈ (NLU) аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•а§ҚаҰ°аҰҝаҰҜаҰјаҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰ¬аҰҝаҰӯаҰҝаҰЁа§ҚаҰЁ аҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҚаҰ•а§ҮаҰ° аҰёаҰҫаҰҘа§Ү аҰёаҰӮаҰҜа§ҒаҰ•а§ҚаҰӨаҘӨ аҰҸаҰҹаҰҝ аҰёа§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰӯаҰҫаҰ¬аҰҝаҰ• аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ¶а§ҚаҰІа§ҮаҰ·аҰЈ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•а§ҚаҰ°аҰҝаҰҜаҰјаҰҫаҰ° аҰёаҰҫаҰҘа§Ү аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӘаҰ°а§ҚаҰ•аҰҝаҰӨ аҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҚаҰ•аҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝаҰ° пҝҪ

title: Dense-ATOMIC: Towards Densely-connected ATOMIC with High Knowledge Coverage and Massive Multi-hop Paths
abstract: ATOMIC is a large-scale commonsense knowledge graph (CSKG) containing everyday if-then knowledge triplets, i.e., head event, relation, tail event. The one-hop annotation manner made ATOMIC a set of independent bipartite graphs, which ignored the numerous links between events in different bipartite graphs and consequently caused shortages in knowledge coverage and multi-hop paths. In this work, we aim to construct Dense-ATOMIC with high knowledge coverage and massive multi-hop paths. The events in ATOMIC are normalized to a consistent pattern at first. We then propose a CSKG completion method called Rel-CSKGC to predict the relation given the head event and the tail event of a triplet, and train a CSKG completion model based on existing triplets in ATOMIC. We finally utilize the model to complete the missing links in ATOMIC and accordingly construct Dense-ATOMIC. Both automatic and human evaluation on an annotated subgraph of ATOMIC demonstrate the advantage of Rel-CSKGC over strong baselines. We further conduct extensive evaluations on Dense-ATOMIC in terms of statistics, human evaluation, and simple downstream tasks, all proving Dense-ATOMICвҖҷs advantages in Knowledge Coverage and Multi-hop Paths. Both the source code of Rel-CSKGC and Dense-ATOMIC are publicly available on https://github.com/NUSTM/Dense-ATOMIC.
question: аҰӘа§ҮаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°аҰҹаҰҝаҰӨа§Ү аҰ•аҰӨаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝ аҰёа§ҖаҰ®аҰҫаҰ¬аҰҰа§ҚаҰ§аҰӨаҰҫ аҰҶаҰІа§ӢаҰҡаҰЁаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?
prompt: "Dense-ATOMIC: Towards Densely-connected ATOMIC with High Knowledge Coverage and Massive Multi-hop Paths" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰҜаҰј Shen,Xiangqing  et al. аҰҰа§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ, аҰӘа§ҮаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°аҰҹаҰҝаҰӨа§Ү аҰ•аҰӨаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝ аҰёа§ҖаҰ®аҰҫаҰ¬аҰҰа§ҚаҰ§аҰӨаҰҫ аҰҶаҰІа§ӢаҰҡаҰЁаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?
output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰҮ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶а§ҚаҰЁа§ҮаҰ° аҰүаҰӨа§ҚаҰӨаҰ° аҰҰа§ҮаҰ“аҰҜаҰјаҰҫаҰ° аҰҡа§ҮаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁаҘӨ аҰёа§ҒаҰӨаҰ°аҰҫаҰӮ, аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶а§ҚаҰЁаҰҹаҰҝ аҰ№аҰІ "Dense-ATOMIC: Towards Densely-connected ATOMIC with High Knowledge Coverage and Massive Multi-hop Paths" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ•а§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ, аҰӘа§ҮаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°а§Ү аҰ•аҰӨаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝ аҰёа§ҖаҰ®аҰҫаҰ¬аҰҰа§ҚаҰ§аҰӨаҰҫ аҰүаҰІа§ҚаҰІа§ҮаҰ– аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү? аҰёа§ҒаҰӨаҰ°аҰҫаҰӮ, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰҮ аҰӘа§ҮаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°а§ҮаҰ° аҰ…аҰӯаҰҝаҰ¬а§ҚаҰҜаҰ•а§ҚаҰӨаҰҝаҰӨа§Ү аҰёа§ҖаҰ®аҰҫаҰ¬аҰҰа§ҚаҰ§аҰӨаҰҫ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӘаҰ°а§ҚаҰ•а§Ү аҰ•аҰӨаҰҹаҰҫ аҰүаҰІа§ҚаҰІа§ҮаҰ– аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү аҰӨаҰҫ аҰ–а§ҒаҰҒаҰңа§Ү аҰ¬а§ҮаҰ° аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§ҮаҘӨ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹа§Ғ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ¬аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁ... аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҘаҰ®аҰӨ, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ° аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү аҰҜа§Ү аҰёа§ҖаҰ®аҰҫаҰ¬аҰҰа§ҚаҰ§аҰӨаҰҫ аҰ№аҰІа§Ӣ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•а§ғаҰӨаҰӘаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·а§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•а§ҢаҰ¶аҰІа§Җ аҰ¬аҰҫ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҜаҰја§ӢаҰңаҰЁа§ҖаҰҜаҰјаҰӨаҰҫ, аҰҜаҰҫ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•аҰҫаҰ°а§ҮаҰ° аҰ¬а§ҲаҰ¶аҰҝаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹа§ҚаҰҜ аҰ¬аҰҫ аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ¶а§ҮаҰ· аҰ•аҰҫаҰңа§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҜаҰја§ӢаҰңаҰЁа§ҖаҰҜаҰј аҰ¬а§ҲаҰ¶аҰҝаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹа§ҚаҰҜаҘӨ аҰҸаҰ–аҰҫаҰЁа§Ү, аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶а§ҚаҰЁаҰҹаҰҝ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӯаҰ¬аҰӨ аҰёа§ҖаҰ®аҰҫаҰ¬аҰҰа§ҚаҰ§аҰӨаҰҫ

title: Dissecting Transformer Length Extrapolation via the Lens of Receptive Field Analysis
abstract: Length extrapolation permits training a transformer language model on short sequences that preserves perplexities when tested on substantially longer sequences.A relative positional embedding design, ALiBi, has had the widest usage to date. We dissect ALiBi via the lens of receptive field analysis empowered by a novel cumulative normalized gradient tool. The concept of receptive field further allows us to modify the vanilla Sinusoidal positional embedding to create Sandwich, the first parameter-free relative positional embedding design that truly length information uses longer than the training sequence. Sandwich shares with KERPLE and T5 the same logarithmic decaying temporal bias pattern with learnable relative positional embeddings; these elucidate future extrapolatable positional embedding design.
question: Positional embeddingаҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§ӢаҰ•а§Ү аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰ¶а§ҚаҰ°а§ҮаҰЈаҰҝаҰӨа§Ү аҰ¬аҰҝаҰӯаҰ•а§ҚаҰӨ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰҜаҰҫаҰҜаҰј?
prompt: "Dissecting Transformer Length Extrapolation via the Lens of Receptive Field Analysis" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰҜаҰј Chi,Ta-Chung  et al. аҰҰа§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ, positional embeddingаҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§ӢаҰ•а§Ү аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰ¶а§ҚаҰ°а§ҮаҰЈаҰҝаҰӨа§Ү аҰ¬аҰҝаҰӯаҰ•а§ҚаҰӨ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰҜаҰҫаҰҜаҰј?
output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰӨаҰҫаҰҮ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰҮ аҰӘаҰҫаҰ а§ҚаҰҜаҰӘа§ҒаҰёа§ҚаҰӨаҰ•а§ҮаҰ° аҰ…аҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰЁаҰӨа§ҒаҰЁ аҰүаҰҡа§ҚаҰҡ-аҰёа§ҖаҰ®аҰҫаҰ¬аҰҰа§ҚаҰ§ аҰҹа§ҚаҰ°аҰҫаҰЁаҰңаҰҝаҰ¶аҰЁ аҰ«аҰҫаҰӮаҰ¶аҰЁ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӘаҰ°а§ҚаҰ•аҰҝаҰӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¬аҰЁа§ҚаҰ§а§Ү аҰӘРҫР·аҰҝаҰ¶аҰЁаҰҫаҰІ аҰҮаҰ®а§ҮаҰң аҰҮаҰЁа§ҚаҰЎа§ҮаҰ•а§ҚаҰёаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝаҰ° аҰ¬аҰҝаҰӯаҰҫаҰңаҰЁ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӘаҰ°а§ҚаҰ•а§Ү аҰҡаҰҝаҰЁа§ҚаҰӨаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§ҮаҘӨ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҘаҰ®а§Ү аҰёа§ҚаҰ®аҰ°аҰЈ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁ аҰҜа§Ү аҰҹа§ҚаҰ°аҰҫаҰЁаҰңаҰҝаҰ¶аҰЁ аҰ«аҰҫаҰӮаҰ¶аҰЁаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝаҰ° аҰ®аҰ§а§ҚаҰҜа§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ§аҰҫаҰЁаҰӨ аҰҹа§ҚаҰ°аҰҫаҰЁаҰңаҰҝаҰ¶аҰЁ аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰёаҰҫаҰ°аҰЈ аҰ…аҰӮаҰ¶ аҰ°аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§ҮаҘӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰёаҰҫаҰ°аҰЈ аҰ…аҰӮаҰ¶аҰҹаҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҫаҰҘаҰ®аҰҝаҰ• аҰ°а§ӮаҰӘаҰҫаҰЁа§ҚаҰӨаҰ°а§ҮаҰ° аҰӘаҰ°а§Ү аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰ¬а§ғаҰ№аҰӨа§ҚаҰӨаҰ° аҰ°а§ӮаҰӘаҰҫаҰЁа§ҚаҰӨаҰ°а§ҮаҰ° аҰҰаҰҝаҰ•а§Ү аҰӘаҰ°аҰҝаҰҡаҰҫаҰІаҰҝаҰӨ аҰ•аҰ°а§Ү, аҰҜаҰҫ аҰҸаҰҹаҰҝаҰ•а§Ү аҰ¬а§ҮаҰ¶аҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰёаҰҫаҰ°аҰҝаҰӨ аҰ•аҰ°а§Ү аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӯаҰ¬аҰӨ аҰ¬а§ҮаҰ¶аҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰёаҰҫаҰ°аҰЈ аҰёа§ӮаҰҡаҰ• аҰёаҰ№ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰёаҰҫаҰ°аҰЈ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰа§ҮаҰҜаҰјаҘӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰёаҰҫаҰ°аҰЈ аҰёа§ӮаҰҡаҰ• аҰ№аҰІа§Ӣ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰёаҰҫаҰ°аҰЈ аҰ…аҰӮаҰ¶а§ҮаҰ° аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰ°аҰҫаҰ®аҰҝаҰҹаҰҫаҰ° аҰҜаҰҫ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӨаҰҝаҰҹаҰҝ аҰёа§ҚаҰӨаҰ°а§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ пҝҪ

title: Minding Language ModelsвҖҷ (Lack of) Theory of Mind: A Plug-and-Play Multi-Character Belief Tracker
abstract: Theory of Mind (ToM)вҖ”the ability to reason about the mental states of other peopleвҖ”is a key element of our social intelligence. Yet, despite their ever more impressive performance, large-scale neural language models still lack basic theory of mind capabilities out-of-the-box. We posit that simply scaling up models will not imbue them with theory of mind due to the inherently symbolic and implicit nature of the phenomenon, and instead investigate an alternative: can we design a decoding-time algorithm that enhances theory of mind of off-the-shelf neural language models without explicit supervision? We present SymbolicToM, a plug-and-play approach to reason about the belief states of multiple characters in reading comprehension tasks via explicit symbolic representation. More concretely, our approach tracks each entityвҖҷs beliefs, their estimation of other entitiesвҖҷ beliefs, and higher-order levels of reasoning, all through graphical representations, allowing for more precise and interpretable reasoning than previous approaches. Empirical results on the well-known ToMi benchmark (Le et al., 2019) demonstrate that SymbolicToM dramatically enhances off-the-shelf neural networksвҖҷ theory of mind in a zero-shot setting while showing robust out-of-distribution performance compared to supervised baselines. Our work also reveals spurious patterns in existing theory of mind benchmarks, emphasizing the importance of out-of-distribution evaluation and methods that do not overfit a particular dataset.
question: аҰӨа§ҒаҰІаҰЁаҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰёа§ҒаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°аҰӯаҰҫаҰҮаҰңаҰЎ аҰ®аҰЎа§ҮаҰІ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰЈ аҰҰа§ҮаҰ“аҰҜаҰјаҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?
prompt: "Minding Language ModelsвҖҷ (Lack of) Theory of Mind: A Plug-and-Play Multi-Character Belief Tracker" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰҜаҰј Sclar,Melanie  et al. аҰҰа§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ, аҰӨа§ҒаҰІаҰЁаҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰёа§ҒаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°аҰӯаҰҫаҰҮаҰңаҰЎ аҰ®аҰЎа§ҮаҰІ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰЈ аҰҰа§ҮаҰ“аҰҜаҰјаҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?
output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰӨаҰҫаҰ№аҰІа§Ү аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰ¬а§ҮаҰ° аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§Ү аҰҜа§Ү Sclar, Melanie аҰҸаҰ° аҰёаҰ№аҰ•аҰҫаҰ°а§ҖаҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰёаҰҫаҰҘа§Ү аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰёа§ҒаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°аҰӯаҰҫаҰҮаҰңаҰЎ аҰ®аҰЎа§ҮаҰІаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰЈа§ҮаҰ° аҰёаҰҫаҰҘа§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰҝаҰӨ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү аҰӨаҰҫ аҰЁаҰҝаҰҜаҰја§Ү аҰҡаҰҝаҰЁа§ҚаҰӨаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ "Minding Language Models' (Lack of) Theory of Mind: A Plug-and-Play Multi-Character Belief Tracker" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҘӨ аҰ№а§ҒаҰ®аҰ®, аҰёа§ҒаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°аҰӯаҰҫаҰҮаҰңаҰЎ аҰ®аҰЎа§ҮаҰІ аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰЈа§ҮаҰ° аҰёаҰ®аҰёа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§Ӣ аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ¬а§ҮаҰҡаҰЁаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫаҰ° аҰёаҰ®аҰҜаҰј аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ° аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰҶаҰёаҰҫ аҰүаҰІа§ҚаҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҜа§ӢаҰ—а§ҚаҰҜ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӨаҰҝаҰҜа§ӢаҰ—а§Җ аҰ№аҰІа§Ӣ аҰёа§ҒаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°аҰӯаҰҫаҰҮаҰңаҰҫаҰ° аҰ•а§ӢаҰЎаҘӨ аҰёа§ҒаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°аҰӯаҰҫаҰҮаҰңаҰҫаҰ° аҰ•а§ӢаҰЎ аҰ№аҰІа§Ӣ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰЈ аҰӘаҰҰа§ҚаҰ§аҰӨаҰҝ аҰҜаҰҫ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰ®аҰЎа§ҮаҰІаҰ•а§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҰаҰӨа§ҚаҰӨ аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫ аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҜаҰ¬аҰ№аҰҫаҰ° аҰ•аҰ°а§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҫаҰҘаҰ®аҰҝаҰ• аҰ…аҰҘаҰ¬аҰҫ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҫаҰҘаҰ®аҰҝаҰ• аҰӯа§ҒаҰІаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝ аҰёаҰӮаҰ¶а§ӢаҰ§аҰЁ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰЈ аҰ•аҰ°а§ҮаҘӨ аҰҸаҰҮ аҰ•а§ҚаҰ·а§ҮаҰӨа§ҚаҰ°а§Ү, аҰёа§ҒаҰӘаҰҫаҰ°аҰӯаҰҫаҰҮаҰңаҰЎ аҰ®аҰЎа§Ү

title: QUEST: A Retrieval Dataset of Entity-Seeking Queries with Implicit Set Operations
abstract: Formulating selective information needs results in queries that implicitly specify set operations, such as intersection, union, and difference. For instance, one might search for вҖңshorebirds that are not sandpipersвҖқ or вҖңscience-fiction films shot in EnglandвҖқ. To study the ability of retrieval systems to meet such information needs, we construct QUEST, a dataset of 3357 natural language queries with implicit set operations, that map to a set of entities corresponding to Wikipedia documents. The dataset challenges models to match multiple constraints mentioned in queries with corresponding evidence in documents and correctly perform various set operations. The dataset is constructed semi-automatically using Wikipedia category names. Queries are automatically composed from individual categories, then paraphrased and further validated for naturalness and fluency by crowdworkers. Crowdworkers also assess the relevance of entities based on their documents and highlight attribution of query constraints to spans of document text. We analyze several modern retrieval systems, finding that they often struggle on such queries. Queries involving negation and conjunction are particularly challenging and systems are further challenged with combinations of these operations.
question: QUEST аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҮаҰҹа§Ү natural language query аҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§Ӣ аҰ•а§ҹаҰҹаҰҝ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҫаҰҜаҰј аҰЁаҰҝаҰ°а§ҚаҰ®аҰҝаҰӨ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?
prompt: "QUEST: A Retrieval Dataset of Entity-Seeking Queries with Implicit Set Operations" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰҜаҰј Malaviya,Chaitanya  et al. аҰҰа§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ, quEST аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҮаҰҹа§Ү natural language query аҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§Ӣ аҰ•а§ҹаҰҹаҰҝ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҫаҰҜаҰј аҰЁаҰҝаҰ°а§ҚаҰ®аҰҝаҰӨ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?
output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰҮ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶а§ҚаҰЁ аҰёаҰ®аҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰЁ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫаҰ° аҰҡа§ҮаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁаҘӨ аҰёа§ҒаҰӨаҰ°аҰҫаҰӮ, аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶а§ҚаҰЁаҰҹаҰҝ аҰ№аҰІ: "quEST аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҮаҰҹа§Ү аҰЁа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰЁаҰҝаҰҜаҰјаҰҫаҰІ аҰІаҰҫаҰҮаҰЁаҰҫаҰ° аҰ•а§ҢаҰ¶аҰІаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝ аҰ•аҰӨаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҫаҰҜаҰј аҰүаҰӘаҰёа§ҚаҰҘаҰҫаҰӘаҰҝаҰӨ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү? " аҰҸаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰ…аҰЁа§ҒаҰёаҰЁа§ҚаҰ§аҰҫаҰЁ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ•аҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰЁаҰҫаҰ® аҰүаҰІа§ҚаҰІа§ҮаҰ– аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү: Malaviya, Chaitanya аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰ…аҰЁа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜаҘӨ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁаҘӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҘаҰ®аҰӨ, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ° аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү аҰҜа§Ү quEST аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҮаҰҹ аҰҜаҰҫ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰЁаҰҝаҰ°а§ҚаҰҰаҰҝаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹ аҰ•аҰҫаҰ аҰҫаҰ®а§Ӣ аҰёаҰ№ аҰЁаҰҝаҰ°а§ҚаҰҰаҰҝаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹ аҰ§аҰ°аҰЈа§ҮаҰ° аҰ•а§ҢаҰ¶аҰІ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӘаҰ°а§ҚаҰ•аҰҝаҰӨ аҰ•а§ҢаҰ¶аҰІ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӘаҰ°а§ҚаҰ•а§Ү аҰҶаҰІа§ӢаҰҡаҰЁаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°а§ҮаҘӨ аҰҸаҰҹаҰҝ аҰЁаҰҝаҰ°а§ҚаҰҰаҰҝаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹ аҰ•а§ҢаҰ¶аҰІ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӘаҰ°а§ҚаҰ•а§Ү аҰҶаҰІа§ӢаҰҡаҰЁаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°а§Ү аҰҜа§ҮаҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§Ӣ аҰёаҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰ°аҰЈ аҰёаҰҫаҰ¬аҰёа§ҚаҰҹа§ҚаҰ°аҰҝаҰӮ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӘаҰ°а§ҚаҰ•а§Ү аҰЁаҰҜаҰј аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰ¬а§ғаҰ№аҰӨа§ҚаҰӨаҰ° аҰёа§ҮаҰҹ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•а§ҚаҰ°аҰҝаҰҜаҰјаҰҫ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӘаҰ°а§ҚаҰ•а§Ү аҰ№аҰҜ

title: Weaker Than You Think: A Critical Look at Weakly Supervised Learning
abstract: Weakly supervised learning is a popular approach for training machine learning models in low-resource settings. Instead of requesting high-quality yet costly human annotations, it allows training models with noisy annotations obtained from various weak sources. Recently, many sophisticated approaches have been proposed for robust training under label noise, reporting impressive results. In this paper, we revisit the setup of these approaches and find that the benefits brought by these approaches are significantly overestimated. Specifically, we find that the success of existing weakly supervised learning approaches heavily relies on the availability of clean validation samples which, as we show, can be leveraged much more efficiently by simply training on them. After using these clean labels in training, the advantages of using these sophisticated approaches are mostly wiped out. This remains true even when reducing the size of the available clean data to just five samples per class, making these approaches impractical. To understand the true value of weakly supervised learning, we thoroughly analyze diverse NLP datasets and tasks to ascertain when and why weakly supervised approaches work. Based on our findings, we provide recommendations for future research.
question: CFT-аҰҸаҰ° аҰӘа§ӮаҰ°а§ҚаҰЈаҰ°а§ӮаҰӘ аҰ•а§Җ аҰҮаҰӮаҰ°а§ҮаҰңаҰҝаҰӨа§Ү ?
prompt: "Weaker Than You Think: A Critical Look at Weakly Supervised Learning" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰҜаҰј Zhu,Dawei  et al. аҰҰа§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ, cfT-аҰҸаҰ° аҰӘа§ӮаҰ°а§ҚаҰЈаҰ°а§ӮаҰӘ аҰ•а§Җ аҰҮаҰӮаҰ°а§ҮаҰңаҰҝаҰӨа§Ү ?
output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰӨаҰҫаҰ№аҰІа§Ү аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү "Weaker Than You Think: A Critical Look at Weakly Supervised Learning" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ•аҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰЎаҰҫаҰ¬аҰІ аҰҸаҰё (dublin core) аҰ«аҰ°а§ҚаҰ®аҰҹаҰҝ аҰ–а§ҒаҰҒаҰңа§Ү аҰ¬а§ҮаҰ° аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§ҮаҘӨ аҰёа§ҒаҰӨаҰ°аҰҫаҰӮ, аҰҸаҰҹаҰҝ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•аҰҫаҰ¶аҰ• аҰЁаҰҝаҰ°а§ҚаҰҰа§ҮаҰ¶аҰҝаҰ•аҰҫ, аҰҜаҰҫ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ§аҰҫаҰЁаҰӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•аҰҫаҰ¶аҰ•а§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰ…аҰ¬аҰёа§ҚаҰҘаҰҫаҰЁ, аҰЁаҰҫаҰ®, аҰҮаҰӨа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰҰаҰҝ аҰүаҰӘаҰёа§ҚаҰҘаҰҫаҰӘаҰЁ аҰ•аҰ°а§ҮаҘӨ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҝ аҰҸаҰҮ аҰ§аҰ°аҰЁа§ҮаҰ° аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰЁаҰҝаҰ°а§ҚаҰҰа§ҮаҰ¶аҰҝаҰ•аҰҫ аҰҘа§ҮаҰ•а§Ү аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁа§Ӣ аҰүа§ҺаҰё аҰңаҰЎаҰјаҰҝаҰӨ аҰ•аҰ°а§ҮаҰӣаҰҝ аҰ•аҰҝаҰЁаҰҫаҘӨ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ° аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү аҰҜа§Ү Dublin Core аҰ«аҰ°а§ҚаҰ®аҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝ аҰ®а§ҢаҰІаҰҝаҰ• аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•аҰҫаҰ¶аҰ• аҰЁаҰҝаҰ°а§ҚаҰҰа§ҮаҰ¶аҰҝаҰ•аҰҫаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝаҰ° аҰ®аҰӨа§Ӣ аҰ•аҰҫаҰң аҰ•аҰ°а§Ү, аҰ•аҰҝаҰЁа§ҚаҰӨа§Ғ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӯаҰ¬аҰӨ аҰҸаҰҹаҰҝ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰӯаҰҝаҰЁа§ҚаҰЁ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӨаҰҝаҰӘаҰҫаҰҰаҰЁаҘӨ аҰҰаҰҫаҰҒаҰЎаҰјаҰҫаҰЁ, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ° аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰ№аҰҜаҰј аҰҸаҰҮ аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ•аҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰЎаҰҫаҰ¬аҰІ аҰҸаҰё аҰ«аҰ°а§ҚаҰ®аҰҹаҰҝ аҰёаҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰ°аҰЈаҰӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҘаҰ® аҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҘаҰ® аҰ…аҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰ° аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰ¶а§Ү

title: Compositional Generalization without Trees using Multiset Tagging and Latent Permutations
abstract: Seq2seq models have been shown to struggle with compositional generalization in semantic parsing, i.e. generalizing to unseen compositions of phenomena that the model handles correctly in isolation. We phrase semantic parsing as a two-step process: we first tag each input token with a multiset of output tokens. Then we arrange the tokens into an output sequence using a new way of parameterizing and predicting permutations. We formulate predicting a permutation as solving a regularized linear program and we backpropagate through the solver. In contrast to prior work, our approach does not place a priori restrictions on possible permutations, making it very expressive. Our model outperforms pretrained seq2seq models and prior work on realistic semantic parsing tasks that require generalization to longer examples. We also outperform non-tree-based models on structural generalization on the COGS benchmark. For the first time, we show that a model without an inductive bias provided by trees achieves high accuracy on generalization to deeper recursion depth.

title: Tell2Design: A Dataset for Language-Guided Floor Plan Generation
abstract: We consider the task of generating designs directly from natural language descriptions, and consider floor plan generation as the initial research area. Language conditional generative models have recently been very successful in generating high-quality artistic images. However, designs must satisfy different constraints that are not present in generating artistic images, particularly spatial and relational constraints. We make multiple contributions to initiate research on this task. First, we introduce a novel dataset, Tell2Design (T2D), which contains more than 80k floor plan designs associated with natural language instructions. Second, we propose a Sequence-to-Sequence model that can serve as a strong baseline for future research. Third, we benchmark this task with several text-conditional image generation models. We conclude by conducting human evaluations on the generated samples and providing an analysis of human performance. We hope our contributions will propel the research on language-guided design generation forward.

output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰҹаҰҫ аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫаҰ° аҰҡа§ҮаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁаҘӨ аҰёаҰ®аҰёа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰҹаҰҝ "Tell2Design: A Dataset for Language-Guided Floor Plan Generation" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶а§ҚаҰЁ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӣа§Ү, аҰҜа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰЁа§Ү Leng, Sicong аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰ…аҰЁа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ•аҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰҰа§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ t2D аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫ аҰёа§ҮаҰҹа§ҮаҰ° аҰӘа§ҒаҰ°а§Ӣ аҰ°а§ӮаҰӘаҰҹаҰҝ аҰ•а§Җ аҰ№аҰІа§Ү аҰӨаҰҫ аҰңаҰҫаҰЁаҰӨа§Ү аҰҡаҰҫаҰҜаҰјаҘӨ аҰ№а§ҒаҰ®аҰ®, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҝ аҰңаҰҫаҰЁаҰҝ аҰҜа§Ү "t2D" аҰ¶аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҰаҰ—а§ҒаҰҡа§ҚаҰӣаҰҹаҰҝ аҰёаҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰ°аҰЈаҰӨ аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫ аҰёа§ҮаҰҹа§ҮаҰ° аҰЁаҰҫаҰ® аҰ№аҰҝаҰёаҰҫаҰ¬а§Ү аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҜаҰ¬аҰ№а§ғаҰӨ аҰ№аҰҜаҰј аҰҜа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰЁа§Ү "t" аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӯаҰ¬аҰӨ "text" аҰ…аҰҘаҰ¬аҰҫ "target"-аҰҸаҰ° аҰёаҰҫаҰҘа§Ү аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӘаҰ°а§ҚаҰ•аҰҝаҰӨ аҰ№аҰӨа§Ү аҰӘаҰҫаҰ°а§Ү, аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ "2D" аҰ¬аҰІаҰӣа§Ү аҰҜа§Ү аҰ«аҰҝаҰІа§ҚаҰЎ аҰӘа§ҚаҰІа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰЁаҰҹаҰҝ 2D аҰёа§ҚаҰҘаҰҫаҰЁаҰҫаҰҷа§ҚаҰ•а§Ү аҰҰа§ҮаҰ“аҰҜаҰјаҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§ҮаҘӨ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰёа§ҚаҰ®аҰ°аҰЈ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁ аҰҜа§Ү аҰҸаҰҮ аҰ§аҰ°аҰЁа§ҮаҰ° аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫ аҰёа§ҮаҰҹаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝ аҰёаҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰ°аҰЈаҰӨ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰүаҰӘаҰҫаҰҰаҰҫаҰЁ аҰ¬аҰҫ аҰүаҰӘаҰҫаҰҰаҰҫаҰЁа§ҮаҰ° аҰёаҰӮаҰ—а§ҚаҰ°аҰ№ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§Ү аҰҘаҰҫаҰ•а§Ү, аҰҜа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰЁа§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӨаҰҝаҰҹаҰҝ аҰүаҰӘ

title: StoryTrans: Non-Parallel Story Author-Style Transfer with Discourse Representations and Content Enhancing
abstract: Non-parallel text style transfer is an important task in natural language generation. However, previous studies concentrate on the token or sentence level, such as sentence sentiment and formality transfer, but neglect long style transfer at the discourse level. Long texts usually involve more complicated author linguistic preferences such as discourse structures than sentences. In this paper, we formulate the task of non-parallel story author-style transfer, which requires transferring an input story into a specified author style while maintaining source semantics. To tackle this problem, we propose a generation model, named StoryTrans, which leverages discourse representations to capture source content information and transfer them to target styles with learnable style embeddings. We use an additional training objective to disentangle stylistic features from the learned discourse representation to prevent the model from degenerating to an auto-encoder. Moreover, to enhance content preservation, we design a mask-and-fill framework to explicitly fuse style-specific keywords of source texts into generation. Furthermore, we constructed new datasets for this task in Chinese and English, respectively. Extensive experiments show that our model outperforms strong baselines in overall performance of style transfer and content preservation.
question: аҰ—аҰ¬а§ҮаҰ·аҰЈаҰҫаҰҹаҰҝаҰӨа§Ү style transfer accuracyаҰ° аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ coefficient аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҜаҰ¬аҰ№аҰҫаҰ° аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?
prompt: "StoryTrans: Non-Parallel Story Author-Style Transfer with Discourse Representations and Content Enhancing" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰҜаҰј Zhu,Xuekai  et al. аҰҰа§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ, аҰ—аҰ¬а§ҮаҰ·аҰЈаҰҫаҰҹаҰҝаҰӨа§Ү style transfer accuracyаҰ° аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ coefficient аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҜаҰ¬аҰ№аҰҫаҰ° аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?
output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰҮ аҰёаҰ®аҰёа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰҹаҰҝ аҰёаҰ®аҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰЁ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫаҰ° аҰҡа§ҮаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁаҘӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶а§ҚаҰЁаҰҹаҰҝ "StoryTrans: Non-Parallel Story Author-Style Transfer with Discourse Representations and Content Enhancing" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ Zhu, Xuekai аҰҸаҰ° аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӨа§ҚаҰҜаҰ•а§ҚаҰ· аҰ—аҰ¬а§ҮаҰ·аҰЈаҰҫаҰӘаҰӨа§ҚаҰ°а§Ү аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰёаҰ№аҰ— аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҜаҰ¬аҰ№аҰҫаҰ° аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү аҰӨаҰҫ аҰңаҰҫаҰЁаҰӨа§Ү аҰҡаҰҫаҰҮаҰӣа§ҮаҘӨ аҰ№а§ҒаҰ®аҰ®, аҰёа§ҒаҰӨаҰ°аҰҫаҰӮ аҰҸаҰҹаҰҝ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰ—аҰ¬а§ҮаҰ·аҰЈаҰҫ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•а§ҚаҰ°аҰҝаҰҜаҰјаҰҫ аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ¬а§ғаҰӨаҰҝ, аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰӨаҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ аҰёаҰ№аҰ—аҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӘаҰ°а§ҚаҰ•а§Ү аҰңаҰҝаҰңа§ҚаҰһаҰҫаҰёаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӣа§ҮаҘӨ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰҹаҰҝ аҰӯа§ҮаҰҷа§Ү аҰ¬аҰІаҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁаҘӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҘаҰ®аҰӨ, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ° аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү аҰҜа§Ү "StoryTrans" аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰ…аҰЁаҰІаҰҫаҰҮаҰЁ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•аҰІа§ҚаҰӘ аҰ№аҰӨа§Ү аҰӘаҰҫаҰ°а§Ү аҰҜа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰЁа§Ү аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҜаҰ¬аҰ№аҰҫаҰ°аҰ•аҰҫаҰ°а§Җ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰЁаҰҝаҰ°а§ҚаҰҰаҰҝаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹ аҰүаҰҰа§ҚаҰҰа§ҮаҰ¶а§ҚаҰҜа§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰЁаҰӨа§ҒаҰЁ аҰ•аҰҫаҰ аҰҫаҰ®а§Ӣ аҰӨа§ҲаҰ°аҰҝ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰӘаҰҫаҰ°а§Ү, аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӯаҰ¬аҰӨ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰ•аҰҫаҰ аҰҫаҰ®а§Ӣ аҰҜаҰҫ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰЁаҰҝаҰ°а§ҚаҰҰаҰҝаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹ аҰүаҰҰа§ҚаҰҰа§ҮаҰ¶а§ҚаҰҜа§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰҸ

title: Hearing Lips in Noise: Universal Viseme-Phoneme Mapping and Transfer for Robust Audio-Visual Speech Recognition
abstract: Audio-visual speech recognition (AVSR) provides a promising solution to ameliorate the noise-robustness of audio-only speech recognition with visual information. However, most existing efforts still focus on audio modality to improve robustness considering its dominance in AVSR task, with noise adaptation techniques such as front-end denoise processing. Though effective, these methods are usually faced with two practical challenges: 1) lack of sufficient labeled noisy audio-visual training data in some real-world scenarios and 2) less optimal model generality to unseen testing noises. In this work, we investigate the noise-invariant visual modality to strengthen robustness of AVSR, which can adapt to any testing noises while without dependence on noisy training data, a.k.a., unsupervised noise adaptation. Inspired by human perception mechanism, we propose a universal viseme-phoneme mapping (UniVPM) approach to implement modality transfer, which can restore clean audio from visual signals to enable speech recognition under any noisy conditions. Extensive experiments on public benchmarks LRS3 and LRS2 show that our approach achieves the state-of-the-art under various noisy as well as clean conditions. In addition, we also outperform previous state-of-the-arts on visual speech recognition task.

title: Knowledge Transfer in Incremental Learning for Multilingual Neural Machine Translation
abstract: In the real-world scenario, a longstanding goal of multilingual neural machine translation (MNMT) is that a single model can incrementally adapt to new language pairs without accessing previous training data. In this scenario, previous studies concentrate on overcoming catastrophic forgetting while lacking encouragement to learn new knowledge from incremental language pairs, especially when the incremental language is not related to the set of original languages. To better acquire new knowledge, we propose a knowledge transfer method that can efficiently adapt original MNMT models to diverse incremental language pairs. The method flexibly introduces the knowledge from an external model into original models, which encourages the models to learn new language pairs, completing the procedure of knowledge transfer. Moreover, all original parameters are frozen to ensure that translation qualities on original language pairs are not degraded. Experimental results show that our method can learn new knowledge from diverse language pairs incrementally meanwhile maintaining performance on original language pairs, outperforming various strong baselines in incremental learning for MNMT.
question: аҰёаҰ®аҰёа§ҚаҰӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰЈ аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҮаҰҹ аҰҘа§ҮаҰ•а§Ү аҰёаҰӮаҰ—а§ғаҰ№а§ҖаҰӨ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?

title: Causes and Cures for Interference in Multilingual Translation
abstract: Multilingual machine translation models can benefit from synergy between different language pairs, but also suffer from interference. While there is a growing number of sophisticated methods that aim to eliminate interference, our understanding of interference as a phenomenon is still limited. This work identifies the main factors that contribute to interference in multilingual machine translation. Through systematic experimentation, we find that interference (or synergy) are primarily determined by model size, data size, and the proportion of each language pair within the total dataset. We observe that substantial interference occurs mainly when the model is very small with respect to the available training data, and that using standard transformer configurations with less than one billion parameters largely alleviates interference and promotes synergy. Moreover, we show that tuning the sampling temperature to control the proportion of each language pair in the data is key to balancing the amount of interference between low and high resource language pairs effectively, and can lead to superior performance overall.
question: аҰ¬аҰ№а§ҒаҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҝаҰ• аҰ…аҰЁа§ҒаҰ¬аҰҫаҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰ•а§ҚаҰ·а§ҮаҰӨа§ҚаҰ°а§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҰаҰӨа§ҚаҰӨ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҫ-аҰңа§ӢаҰЎаҰјаҰҫаҰ° аҰ•аҰҫаҰ°а§ҚаҰҜаҰ•аҰҫаҰ°аҰҝаҰӨаҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӯаҰҫаҰ¬аҰҝаҰӨ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰӘаҰҫаҰ°а§Ү аҰ¬аҰІа§Ү аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰӘаҰҫаҰҒаҰҡаҰҹаҰҝ аҰүаҰӘаҰҫаҰҰаҰҫаҰЁ аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ¬а§ҮаҰҡаҰЁаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?
prompt: "Causes and Cures for Interference in Multilingual Translation" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰҜаҰј Shaham,Uri  et al. аҰҰа§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ, аҰ¬аҰ№а§ҒаҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҝаҰ• аҰ…аҰЁа§ҒаҰ¬аҰҫаҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰ•а§ҚаҰ·а§ҮаҰӨа§ҚаҰ°а§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҰаҰӨа§ҚаҰӨ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҫ-аҰңа§ӢаҰЎаҰјаҰҫаҰ° аҰ•аҰҫаҰ°а§ҚаҰҜаҰ•аҰҫаҰ°аҰҝаҰӨаҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӯаҰҫаҰ¬аҰҝаҰӨ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰӘаҰҫаҰ°а§Ү аҰ¬аҰІа§Ү аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰӘаҰҫаҰҒаҰҡаҰҹаҰҝ аҰүаҰӘаҰҫаҰҰаҰҫаҰЁ аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ¬а§ҮаҰҡаҰЁаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү ?
output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰӨаҰҫаҰҮ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰҮ аҰёаҰ®аҰёа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰҹаҰҝ аҰёаҰ®аҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰЁ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§Ү: "Causes and Cures for Interference in Multilingual Translation" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ, аҰёа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰЁа§Ү аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ¬а§ҮаҰҡаҰҝаҰӨ аҰӘаҰҫаҰҒаҰҡаҰҹаҰҝ аҰүаҰӘаҰҫаҰҰаҰҫаҰЁ аҰ•а§Җ? аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ• аҰ№аҰІ Shaham, Uri аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰ…аҰЁа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜаҘӨ аҰ№а§ҒаҰ®аҰ®, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹа§Ғ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ¬аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁаҘӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҘаҰ®аҰӨ, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ° аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү аҰҜа§Ү "causes and cures" аҰ¶аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҰаҰ—а§ҒаҰҡа§ҚаҰӣаҰҹаҰҝ аҰёаҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰ°аҰЈаҰӨ аҰ¬аҰҝаҰӯаҰҝаҰЁа§ҚаҰЁ аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ·аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҜаҰ¬аҰ№а§ғаҰӨ аҰ№аҰҜаҰј, аҰҜа§ҮаҰ®аҰЁ аҰёаҰ®аҰёа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§ӢаҰ° аҰ•аҰҫаҰ°аҰЈ аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰёаҰ®аҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰЁаҘӨ аҰҸаҰ–аҰҫаҰЁа§Ү аҰёаҰ®аҰёа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰҹаҰҝ аҰ№аҰІа§Ӣ аҰ¬аҰ№а§ҒаҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҝаҰ• аҰ…аҰЁа§ҒаҰ¬аҰҫаҰҰа§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҰаҰӨа§ҚаҰӨ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҫ-аҰңа§ӢаҰЎаҰјаҰҫаҰ° аҰ•аҰҫаҰ°аҰЈа§Ү аҰҳаҰҹаҰҫ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӯаҰҫаҰ¬аҘӨ аҰёа§ҒаҰӨаҰ°аҰҫаҰӮ, аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶а§ҚаҰЁаҰҹаҰҝ аҰ№аҰІа§Ӣ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁ аҰӘаҰҫаҰҒаҰҡаҰҹаҰҝ аҰүаҰӘаҰҫаҰҰаҰҫаҰЁ аҰҜаҰҫ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҰаҰӨа§ҚаҰӨ аҰӯаҰҫаҰ·аҰҫ-аҰңа§ӢаҰЎаҰјаҰҫаҰ° аҰ•аҰҫаҰ°аҰЈа§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӯаҰҫаҰ¬ аҰ«а§ҮаҰІа§Ү, аҰёа§ҮаҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§ӢаҰ•а§Ү аҰӨаҰҫаҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰ…аҰ¬аҰҰаҰҫаҰЁ аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ¶а§ҚаҰІа§ҮаҰ·аҰЈ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҜаҰ¬

title: Scaling in Cognitive Modelling: a Multilingual Approach to Human Reading Times
abstract: Neural language models are increasingly valued in computational psycholinguistics, due to their ability to provide conditional probability distributions over the lexicon that are predictive of human processing times. Given the vast array of available models, it is of both theoretical and methodological importance to assess what features of a model influence its psychometric quality. In this work we focus on parameter size, showing that larger Transformer-based language models generate probabilistic estimates that are less predictive of early eye-tracking measurements reflecting lexical access and early semantic integration. However, relatively bigger models show an advantage in capturing late eye-tracking measurements that reflect the full semantic and syntactic integration of a word into the current language context. Our results are supported by eye movement data in ten languages and consider four models, spanning from 564M to 4.5B parameters.
question: аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ¬а§ҮаҰҡаҰҝаҰӨ аҰ®аҰЎа§ҮаҰІаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝаҰӨа§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰ°аҰҫаҰ®аҰҝаҰҹаҰҫаҰ°а§ҮаҰ° аҰёа§ҖаҰ®аҰҫ аҰ•аҰӨ ?
prompt: "Scaling in Cognitive Modelling: a Multilingual Approach to Human Reading Times" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰҜаҰј de Varda,Andrea  et al. аҰҰа§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ, аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ¬а§ҮаҰҡаҰҝаҰӨ аҰ®аҰЎа§ҮаҰІаҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝаҰӨа§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰ°аҰҫаҰ®аҰҝаҰҹаҰҫаҰ°а§ҮаҰ° аҰёа§ҖаҰ®аҰҫ аҰ•аҰӨ ?
output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰҶаҰёа§ҒаҰЁ аҰҸаҰҮ аҰёаҰ®аҰёа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰҹаҰҝ аҰ§аҰҫаҰӘа§Ү аҰ§аҰҫаҰӘа§Ү аҰёаҰ®аҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰЁ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҝаҘӨ аҰёа§ҒаҰӨаҰ°аҰҫаҰӮ, аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶а§ҚаҰЁаҰҹаҰҝ "Scaling in Cognitive Modeling: A Multilingual Approach to Human Reading Times" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ•аҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰ®аҰЎа§ҮаҰІ аҰӨа§ҲаҰ°аҰҝаҰ° аҰёаҰ®аҰҜаҰј аҰӘа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰ°аҰҫаҰ®аҰҝаҰҹаҰҫаҰ°а§ҮаҰ° аҰёа§ҖаҰ®аҰҫ аҰ•аҰӨ аҰңаҰҫаҰЁаҰӨа§Ү аҰҡаҰҫаҰҮаҰӣа§ҮаҘӨ аҰүаҰӨа§ҚаҰӨаҰ°аҰҹаҰҝ аҰ№аҰІа§Ӣ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰёаҰӮаҰ–а§ҚаҰҜаҰҫ, аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӯаҰ¬аҰӨ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰёа§ҚаҰҘаҰҫаҰЁаҰҫаҰҷа§ҚаҰ• аҰ…аҰҘаҰ¬аҰҫ аҰ•аҰҝаҰӣа§Ғ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӨаҰҝаҰӘаҰҫаҰҰаҰЁа§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰёа§ӮаҰӨа§ҚаҰ°аҘӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҘаҰ®аҰӨ, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ° аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§Ү аҰҜа§Ү аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰ•аҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰ•аҰҫаҰӣа§Ү аҰ•а§Җ аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ·аҰҜаҰј аҰӣаҰҝаҰІ аҰӨаҰҫ аҰ¬аҰҝаҰ¶а§ҚаҰІа§ҮаҰ·аҰЈ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰүаҰҡаҰҝаҰӨаҘӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ¶а§ҚаҰЁаҰҹаҰҝ аҰӘа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰ°аҰҫаҰ®аҰҝаҰҹаҰҫаҰ°а§ҮаҰ° аҰёа§ҖаҰ®аҰҫ аҰңаҰҫаҰЁаҰӨа§Ү аҰҡаҰҫаҰҮаҰӣа§Ү, аҰӨаҰҫаҰҮ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӯаҰ¬аҰӨ аҰӨаҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ аҰ•а§ӢаҰЁа§Ӣ аҰ®аҰЎа§ҮаҰІ аҰӨа§ҲаҰ°аҰҝ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӣа§Ү аҰҜа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰЁа§Ү аҰӘа§ҚаҰҜаҰҫаҰ°аҰҫаҰ®аҰҝаҰҹаҰҫаҰ°аҰ—а§ҒаҰІаҰҝ аҰёа§ҖаҰ®аҰҫаҰ¬аҰҰа§ҚаҰ§ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰја§ҮаҰӣа§Ү? аҰ…аҰҘаҰ¬аҰҫ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӯаҰ¬аҰӨ аҰӨаҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰӨаҰҝаҰӘаҰҫаҰҰаҰЁа§ҮаҰ° аҰ®аҰ§а§ҚаҰҜа§Ү аҰ•аҰҝаҰӣа§Ғ аҰёа§ӮаҰӨа§ҚаҰ° аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҜаҰ¬аҰ№аҰҫаҰ° пҝҪ

title: Considerations for meaningful sign language machine translation based on glosses
abstract: Automatic sign language processing is gaining popularity in Natural Language Processing (NLP) research (Yin et al., 2021). In machine translation (MT) in particular, sign language translation based on glosses is a prominent approach. In this paper, we review recent works on neural gloss translation. We find that limitations of glosses in general and limitations of specific datasets are not discussed in a transparent manner and that there is no common standard for evaluation. To address these issues, we put forward concrete recommendations for future research on gloss translation. Our suggestions advocate awareness of the inherent limitations of gloss-based approaches, realistic datasets, stronger baselines and convincing evaluation.
question: аҰ—а§ҚаҰІаҰё аҰҶаҰүаҰҹаҰӘа§ҒаҰҹа§Ү BLEU аҰ—аҰЈаҰЁаҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰёа§ҚаҰӨаҰҫаҰ¬аҰҝаҰӨ аҰӘаҰҰа§ҚаҰ§аҰӨаҰҝ аҰ•а§Җ ?
prompt: "Considerations for meaningful sign language machine translation based on glosses" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰІа§ҮаҰ–аҰҫаҰҜаҰј MГјller,Mathias  et al. аҰҰа§ҚаҰ¬аҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ, аҰ—а§ҚаҰІаҰё аҰҶаҰүаҰҹаҰӘа§ҒаҰҹа§Ү BLEU аҰ—аҰЈаҰЁаҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰёа§ҚаҰӨаҰҫаҰ¬аҰҝаҰӨ аҰӘаҰҰа§ҚаҰ§аҰӨаҰҝ аҰ•а§Җ ?
output_text: thinkаҰ аҰҝаҰ• аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү, аҰёа§ҒаҰӨаҰ°аҰҫаҰӮ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰ¬аҰІаҰӨа§Ү аҰ№аҰ¬а§Ү аҰҜа§Ү MГјller аҰҸаҰ¬аҰӮ аҰӨаҰҫаҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰёаҰ№аҰ•аҰҫаҰ°а§ҖаҰҰа§ҮаҰ° аҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§ҮаҰ° аҰҸаҰҮ аҰӘаҰҫаҰ а§ҚаҰҜаҰӘа§ҒаҰёа§ҚаҰӨаҰ•а§Ү "аҰҜа§ҒаҰ•а§ҚаҰӨаҰҝаҰ° аҰүа§ҺаҰӘаҰҫаҰҰаҰЁа§ҮаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰ—а§ҚаҰІа§ӢаҰң аҰЎа§ҮаҰҹаҰҫаҰёа§ҮаҰҹа§ҮаҰ° аҰ®аҰҫаҰ§а§ҚаҰҜаҰ®а§Ү аҰ®аҰҫаҰЁаҰҫаҰЁаҰёаҰҮ аҰёа§ҒаҰ¬аҰҝаҰ§аҰҫ аҰёаҰ®а§ҚаҰӘаҰ°а§ҚаҰ•а§Ү аҰ…аҰЁа§ҒаҰ®аҰҫаҰЁ" аҰ¶аҰҝаҰ°а§ӢаҰЁаҰҫаҰ®а§Ү, аҰӨаҰҫаҰ°аҰҫ аҰ•а§ҖаҰӯаҰҫаҰ¬а§Ү BLEU аҰ—аҰЈаҰЁаҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰёа§ҚаҰӨаҰҫаҰ¬ аҰ•аҰ°а§ҮаҘӨ аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ•а§Ү аҰҸаҰҮ аҰ•аҰҫаҰҷа§ҚаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰҝаҰӨ аҰ…аҰӮаҰ¶аҰҹаҰҝ аҰ–а§ҒаҰҒаҰңа§Ү аҰ¬а§ҮаҰ° аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫаҰ° аҰҡа§ҮаҰ·а§ҚаҰҹаҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰӨа§Ү аҰҰаҰҝаҰЁаҘӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰҘаҰ®аҰӨ, аҰҶаҰ®аҰҫаҰ° аҰ®аҰЁа§Ү аҰҶаҰӣа§Ү аҰҜа§Ү аҰ¬а§ҮаҰІа§ҒаҰҜаҰја§ҚаҰҜа§Ғ (BLEU) аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰӘаҰҰа§ҚаҰ§аҰӨаҰҝ аҰҜаҰҫ аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҜаҰ¬аҰ№аҰҫаҰ° аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰј аҰёа§ҒаҰ¬аҰҝаҰ§аҰҫаҰ—а§ҒаҰІа§ӢаҰ•а§Ү аҰ®аҰҫаҰЁаҰҫаҰЁаҰёаҰҮ аҰ•аҰҝаҰЁаҰҫ аҰӨаҰҫ аҰӘаҰ°а§ҖаҰ•а§ҚаҰ·аҰҫ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫаҰ° аҰңаҰЁа§ҚаҰҜаҘӨ аҰҸаҰҹаҰҝ аҰёаҰҫаҰ§аҰҫаҰ°аҰЈаҰӨ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰ®а§ҒаҰ•а§ҚаҰӨ аҰӘа§ҚаҰ°аҰ•аҰҫаҰ° аҰ№аҰҝаҰёаҰҫаҰ¬а§Ү аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҜаҰ¬аҰ№а§ғаҰӨ аҰ№аҰҜаҰј, аҰ•аҰҝаҰЁа§ҚаҰӨа§Ғ аҰҜаҰ–аҰЁ аҰҸаҰ•аҰҹаҰҝ аҰёа§ҒаҰ¬аҰҝаҰ§аҰҫаҰ° аҰүа§ҺаҰӘаҰҫаҰҰаҰЁ аҰ•аҰ°аҰҫ аҰ№аҰҜаҰј аҰҜаҰҫ аҰ—а§ҚаҰІа§ӢаҰң аҰ¬а§ҚаҰҜаҰ¬

title: Question-Answering in a Low-resourced Language: Benchmark Dataset and Models for Tigrinya
abstract: Question-Answering (QA) has seen significant advances recently, achieving near human-level performance over some benchmarks. However, these advances focus on high-resourced languages such as English, while the task remains unexplored for most other languages, mainly due to the lack of annotated datasets. This work presents a native QA dataset for an East African language, Tigrinya. The dataset contains 10.6K question-answer pairs spanning 572 paragraphs extracted from 290 news articles on various topics. The dataset construction method is discussed, which is applicable to constructing similar resources for related languages. We present comprehensive experiments and analyses of several resource-efficient approaches to QA, including monolingual, cross-lingual, and multilingual setups, along with comparisons against machine-translated silver data. Our strong baseline models reach 76% in the F1 score, while the estimated human performance is 92%, indicating that the benchmark presents a good challenge for future work. We make the dataset, models, and leaderboard publicly available.

title: Transfer and Active Learning for Dissonance Detection: Addressing the Rare-Class Challenge
abstract: While transformer-based systems have enabled greater accuracies with fewer training examples, data acquisition obstacles still persist for rare-class tasks вҖ“ when the class label is very infrequent (e.g. < 5% of samples). Active learning has in general been proposed to alleviate such challenges, but choice of selection strategy, the criteria by which rare-class examples are chosen, has not been systematically evaluated. Further, transformers enable iterative transfer-learning approaches. We propose and investigate transfer- and active learning solutions to the rare class problem of dissonance detection through utilizing models trained on closely related tasks and the evaluation of acquisition strategies, including a proposed probability-of-rare-class (PRC) approach. We perform these experiments for a specific rare-class problem: collecting language samples of cognitive dissonance from social media. We find that PRC is a simple and effective strategy to guide annotations and ultimately improve model accuracy while transfer-learning in a specific order can improve the cold-start performance of the learner but does not benefit iterations of active learning.

title: Generalizing Backpropagation for Gradient-Based Interpretability
abstract: Many popular feature-attribution methods for interpreting deep neural networks rely on computing the gradients of a modelвҖҷs output with respect to its inputs. While these methods can indicate which input features may be important for the modelвҖҷs prediction, they reveal little about the inner workings of the model itself. In this paper, we observe that the gradient computation of a model is a special case of a more general formulation using semirings. This observation allows us to generalize the backpropagation algorithm to efficiently compute other interpretable statistics about the gradient graph of a neural network, such as the highest-weighted path and entropy. We implement this generalized algorithm, evaluate it on synthetic datasets to better understand the statistics it computes, and apply it to study BERTвҖҷs behavior on the subjectвҖ“verb number agreement task (SVA). With this method, we (a) validate that the amount of gradient flow through a component of a model reflects its importance to a prediction and (b) for SVA, identify which pathways of the self-attention mechanism are most important.

title: Resolving Indirect Referring Expressions for Entity Selection
abstract: Recent advances in language modeling have enabled new conversational systems. In particular, it is often desirable for people to make choices among specified options when using such systems. We address the problem of reference resolution, when people use natural expressions to choose between real world entities. For example, given the choice вҖҳShould we make a Simnel cake or a Pandan cakeВҝ a natural response from a non-expert may be indirect: вҖҳletвҖҷs make the green oneвҖҳ. Reference resolution has been little studied with natural expressions, thus robustly understanding such language has large potential for improving naturalness in dialog, recommendation, and search systems. We create AltEntities (Alternative Entities), a new public dataset of entity pairs and utterances, and develop models for the disambiguation problem. Consisting of 42K indirect referring expressions across three domains, it enables for the first time the study of how large language models can be adapted to this task. We find they achieve 82%-87% accuracy in realistic settings, which while reasonable also invites further advances.

title: WikiBio: a Semantic Resource for the Intersectional Analysis of Biographical Events
abstract: Biographical event detection is a relevant task that allows for the exploration and comparison of the ways in which peopleвҖҷs lives are told and represented. This may support several real-life applications in digital humanities and in works aimed at exploring bias about minoritized groups. Despite that, there are no corpora and models specifically designed for this task. In this paper we fill this gap by presenting a new corpus annotated for biographical event detection. The corpus, which includes 20 Wikipedia biographies, was aligned with 5 existing corpora in order to train a model for the biographical event detection task. The model was able to detect all mentions of the target-entity in a biography with an F-score of 0.808 and the entity-related events with an F-score of 0.859. Finally, the model was used for performing an analysis of biases about women and non-Western people in Wikipedia biographies.

title: Hybrid Transducer and Attention based Encoder-Decoder Modeling for Speech-to-Text Tasks
abstract: Transducer and Attention based Encoder-Decoder (AED) are two widely used frameworks for speech-to-text tasks. They are designed for different purposes and each has its own benefits and drawbacks for speech-to-text tasks. In order to leverage strengths of both modeling methods, we propose a solution by combining Transducer and Attention based Encoder-Decoder (TAED) for speech-to-text tasks. The new method leverages AEDвҖҷs strength in non-monotonic sequence to sequence learning while retaining TransducerвҖҷs streaming property. In the proposed framework, Transducer and AED share the same speech encoder. The predictor in Transducer is replaced by the decoder in the AED model, and the outputs of the decoder are conditioned on the speech inputs instead of outputs from an unconditioned language model. The proposed solution ensures that the model is optimized by covering all possible read/write scenarios and creates a matched environment for streaming applications. We evaluate the proposed approach on the MuST-C dataset and the findings demonstrate that TAED performs significantly better than Transducer for offline automatic speech recognition (ASR) and speech-to-text translation (ST) tasks. In the streaming case, TAED outperforms Transducer in the ASR task and one ST direction while comparable results are achieved in another translation direction.

title: WhatвҖҷs the Meaning of Superhuman Performance in TodayвҖҷs NLU?
abstract: In the last five years, there has been a significant focus in Natural Language Processing (NLP) on developing larger Pretrained Language Models (PLMs) and introducing benchmarks such as SuperGLUE and SQuAD to measure their abilities in language understanding, reasoning, and reading comprehension. These PLMs have achieved impressive results on these benchmarks, even surpassing human performance in some cases. This has led to claims of superhuman capabilities and the provocative idea that certain tasks have been solved. In this position paper, we take a critical look at these claims and ask whether PLMs truly have superhuman abilities and what the current benchmarks are really evaluating. We show that these benchmarks have serious limitations affecting the comparison between humans and PLMs and provide recommendations for fairer and more transparent benchmarks.

title: Dense-ATOMIC: Towards Densely-connected ATOMIC with High Knowledge Coverage and Massive Multi-hop Paths
abstract: ATOMIC is a large-scale commonsense knowledge graph (CSKG) containing everyday if-then knowledge triplets, i.e., head event, relation, tail event. The one-hop annotation manner made ATOMIC a set of independent bipartite graphs, which ignored the numerous links between events in different bipartite graphs and consequently caused shortages in knowledge coverage and multi-hop paths. In this work, we aim to construct Dense-ATOMIC with high knowledge coverage and massive multi-hop paths. The events in ATOMIC are normalized to a consistent pattern at first. We then propose a CSKG completion method called Rel-CSKGC to predict the relation given the head event and the tail event of a triplet, and train a CSKG completion model based on existing triplets in ATOMIC. We finally utilize the model to complete the missing links in ATOMIC and accordingly construct Dense-ATOMIC. Both automatic and human evaluation on an annotated subgraph of ATOMIC demonstrate the advantage of Rel-CSKGC over strong baselines. We further conduct extensive evaluations on Dense-ATOMIC in terms of statistics, human evaluation, and simple downstream tasks, all proving Dense-ATOMICвҖҷs advantages in Knowledge Coverage and Multi-hop Paths. Both the source code of Rel-CSKGC and Dense-ATOMIC are publicly available on https://github.com/NUSTM/Dense-ATOMIC.

title: Dissecting Transformer Length Extrapolation via the Lens of Receptive Field Analysis
abstract: Length extrapolation permits training a transformer language model on short sequences that preserves perplexities when tested on substantially longer sequences.A relative positional embedding design, ALiBi, has had the widest usage to date. We dissect ALiBi via the lens of receptive field analysis empowered by a novel cumulative normalized gradient tool. The concept of receptive field further allows us to modify the vanilla Sinusoidal positional embedding to create Sandwich, the first parameter-free relative positional embedding design that truly length information uses longer than the training sequence. Sandwich shares with KERPLE and T5 the same logarithmic decaying temporal bias pattern with learnable relative positional embeddings; these elucidate future extrapolatable positional embedding design.

title: Minding Language ModelsвҖҷ (Lack of) Theory of Mind: A Plug-and-Play Multi-Character Belief Tracker
abstract: Theory of Mind (ToM)вҖ”the ability to reason about the mental states of other peopleвҖ”is a key element of our social intelligence. Yet, despite their ever more impressive performance, large-scale neural language models still lack basic theory of mind capabilities out-of-the-box. We posit that simply scaling up models will not imbue them with theory of mind due to the inherently symbolic and implicit nature of the phenomenon, and instead investigate an alternative: can we design a decoding-time algorithm that enhances theory of mind of off-the-shelf neural language models without explicit supervision? We present SymbolicToM, a plug-and-play approach to reason about the belief states of multiple characters in reading comprehension tasks via explicit symbolic representation. More concretely, our approach tracks each entityвҖҷs beliefs, their estimation of other entitiesвҖҷ beliefs, and higher-order levels of reasoning, all through graphical representations, allowing for more precise and interpretable reasoning than previous approaches. Empirical results on the well-known ToMi benchmark (Le et al., 2019) demonstrate that SymbolicToM dramatically enhances off-the-shelf neural networksвҖҷ theory of mind in a zero-shot setting while showing robust out-of-distribution performance compared to supervised baselines. Our work also reveals spurious patterns in existing theory of mind benchmarks, emphasizing the importance of out-of-distribution evaluation and methods that do not overfit a particular dataset.

title: QUEST: A Retrieval Dataset of Entity-Seeking Queries with Implicit Set Operations
abstract: Formulating selective information needs results in queries that implicitly specify set operations, such as intersection, union, and difference. For instance, one might search for вҖңshorebirds that are not sandpipersвҖқ or вҖңscience-fiction films shot in EnglandвҖқ. To study the ability of retrieval systems to meet such information needs, we construct QUEST, a dataset of 3357 natural language queries with implicit set operations, that map to a set of entities corresponding to Wikipedia documents. The dataset challenges models to match multiple constraints mentioned in queries with corresponding evidence in documents and correctly perform various set operations. The dataset is constructed semi-automatically using Wikipedia category names. Queries are automatically composed from individual categories, then paraphrased and further validated for naturalness and fluency by crowdworkers. Crowdworkers also assess the relevance of entities based on their documents and highlight attribution of query constraints to spans of document text. We analyze several modern retrieval systems, finding that they often struggle on such queries. Queries involving negation and conjunction are particularly challenging and systems are further challenged with combinations of these operations.

title: Weaker Than You Think: A Critical Look at Weakly Supervised Learning
abstract: Weakly supervised learning is a popular approach for training machine learning models in low-resource settings. Instead of requesting high-quality yet costly human annotations, it allows training models with noisy annotations obtained from various weak sources. Recently, many sophisticated approaches have been proposed for robust training under label noise, reporting impressive results. In this paper, we revisit the setup of these approaches and find that the benefits brought by these approaches are significantly overestimated. Specifically, we find that the success of existing weakly supervised learning approaches heavily relies on the availability of clean validation samples which, as we show, can be leveraged much more efficiently by simply training on them. After using these clean labels in training, the advantages of using these sophisticated approaches are mostly wiped out. This remains true even when reducing the size of the available clean data to just five samples per class, making these approaches impractical. To understand the true value of weakly supervised learning, we thoroughly analyze diverse NLP datasets and tasks to ascertain when and why weakly supervised approaches work. Based on our findings, we provide recommendations for future research.

title: Compositional Generalization without Trees using Multiset Tagging and Latent Permutations
abstract: Seq2seq models have been shown to struggle with compositional generalization in semantic parsing, i.e. generalizing to unseen compositions of phenomena that the model handles correctly in isolation. We phrase semantic parsing as a two-step process: we first tag each input token with a multiset of output tokens. Then we arrange the tokens into an output sequence using a new way of parameterizing and predicting permutations. We formulate predicting a permutation as solving a regularized linear program and we backpropagate through the solver. In contrast to prior work, our approach does not place a priori restrictions on possible permutations, making it very expressive. Our model outperforms pretrained seq2seq models and prior work on realistic semantic parsing tasks that require generalization to longer examples. We also outperform non-tree-based models on structural generalization on the COGS benchmark. For the first time, we show that a model without an inductive bias provided by trees achieves high accuracy on generalization to deeper recursion depth.

title: Tell2Design: A Dataset for Language-Guided Floor Plan Generation
abstract: We consider the task of generating designs directly from natural language descriptions, and consider floor plan generation as the initial research area. Language conditional generative models have recently been very successful in generating high-quality artistic images. However, designs must satisfy different constraints that are not present in generating artistic images, particularly spatial and relational constraints. We make multiple contributions to initiate research on this task. First, we introduce a novel dataset, Tell2Design (T2D), which contains more than 80k floor plan designs associated with natural language instructions. Second, we propose a Sequence-to-Sequence model that can serve as a strong baseline for future research. Third, we benchmark this task with several text-conditional image generation models. We conclude by conducting human evaluations on the generated samples and providing an analysis of human performance. We hope our contributions will propel the research on language-guided design generation forward.

title: StoryTrans: Non-Parallel Story Author-Style Transfer with Discourse Representations and Content Enhancing
abstract: Non-parallel text style transfer is an important task in natural language generation. However, previous studies concentrate on the token or sentence level, such as sentence sentiment and formality transfer, but neglect long style transfer at the discourse level. Long texts usually involve more complicated author linguistic preferences such as discourse structures than sentences. In this paper, we formulate the task of non-parallel story author-style transfer, which requires transferring an input story into a specified author style while maintaining source semantics. To tackle this problem, we propose a generation model, named StoryTrans, which leverages discourse representations to capture source content information and transfer them to target styles with learnable style embeddings. We use an additional training objective to disentangle stylistic features from the learned discourse representation to prevent the model from degenerating to an auto-encoder. Moreover, to enhance content preservation, we design a mask-and-fill framework to explicitly fuse style-specific keywords of source texts into generation. Furthermore, we constructed new datasets for this task in Chinese and English, respectively. Extensive experiments show that our model outperforms strong baselines in overall performance of style transfer and content preservation.

title: Hearing Lips in Noise: Universal Viseme-Phoneme Mapping and Transfer for Robust Audio-Visual Speech Recognition
abstract: Audio-visual speech recognition (AVSR) provides a promising solution to ameliorate the noise-robustness of audio-only speech recognition with visual information. However, most existing efforts still focus on audio modality to improve robustness considering its dominance in AVSR task, with noise adaptation techniques such as front-end denoise processing. Though effective, these methods are usually faced with two practical challenges: 1) lack of sufficient labeled noisy audio-visual training data in some real-world scenarios and 2) less optimal model generality to unseen testing noises. In this work, we investigate the noise-invariant visual modality to strengthen robustness of AVSR, which can adapt to any testing noises while without dependence on noisy training data, a.k.a., unsupervised noise adaptation. Inspired by human perception mechanism, we propose a universal viseme-phoneme mapping (UniVPM) approach to implement modality transfer, which can restore clean audio from visual signals to enable speech recognition under any noisy conditions. Extensive experiments on public benchmarks LRS3 and LRS2 show that our approach achieves the state-of-the-art under various noisy as well as clean conditions. In addition, we also outperform previous state-of-the-arts on visual speech recognition task.

title: Knowledge Transfer in Incremental Learning for Multilingual Neural Machine Translation
abstract: In the real-world scenario, a longstanding goal of multilingual neural machine translation (MNMT) is that a single model can incrementally adapt to new language pairs without accessing previous training data. In this scenario, previous studies concentrate on overcoming catastrophic forgetting while lacking encouragement to learn new knowledge from incremental language pairs, especially when the incremental language is not related to the set of original languages. To better acquire new knowledge, we propose a knowledge transfer method that can efficiently adapt original MNMT models to diverse incremental language pairs. The method flexibly introduces the knowledge from an external model into original models, which encourages the models to learn new language pairs, completing the procedure of knowledge transfer. Moreover, all original parameters are frozen to ensure that translation qualities on original language pairs are not degraded. Experimental results show that our method can learn new knowledge from diverse language pairs incrementally meanwhile maintaining performance on original language pairs, outperforming various strong baselines in incremental learning for MNMT.

title: Causes and Cures for Interference in Multilingual Translation
abstract: Multilingual machine translation models can benefit from synergy between different language pairs, but also suffer from interference. While there is a growing number of sophisticated methods that aim to eliminate interference, our understanding of interference as a phenomenon is still limited. This work identifies the main factors that contribute to interference in multilingual machine translation. Through systematic experimentation, we find that interference (or synergy) are primarily determined by model size, data size, and the proportion of each language pair within the total dataset. We observe that substantial interference occurs mainly when the model is very small with respect to the available training data, and that using standard transformer configurations with less than one billion parameters largely alleviates interference and promotes synergy. Moreover, we show that tuning the sampling temperature to control the proportion of each language pair in the data is key to balancing the amount of interference between low and high resource language pairs effectively, and can lead to superior performance overall.

title: Scaling in Cognitive Modelling: a Multilingual Approach to Human Reading Times
abstract: Neural language models are increasingly valued in computational psycholinguistics, due to their ability to provide conditional probability distributions over the lexicon that are predictive of human processing times. Given the vast array of available models, it is of both theoretical and methodological importance to assess what features of a model influence its psychometric quality. In this work we focus on parameter size, showing that larger Transformer-based language models generate probabilistic estimates that are less predictive of early eye-tracking measurements reflecting lexical access and early semantic integration. However, relatively bigger models show an advantage in capturing late eye-tracking measurements that reflect the full semantic and syntactic integration of a word into the current language context. Our results are supported by eye movement data in ten languages and consider four models, spanning from 564M to 4.5B parameters.

title: Considerations for meaningful sign language machine translation based on glosses
abstract: Automatic sign language processing is gaining popularity in Natural Language Processing (NLP) research (Yin et al., 2021). In machine translation (MT) in particular, sign language translation based on glosses is a prominent approach. In this paper, we review recent works on neural gloss translation. We find that limitations of glosses in general and limitations of specific datasets are not discussed in a transparent manner and that there is no common standard for evaluation. To address these issues, we put forward concrete recommendations for future research on gloss translation. Our suggestions advocate awareness of the inherent limitations of gloss-based approaches, realistic datasets, stronger baselines and convincing evaluation.
